title: glandularia radicans
intro: Glandularia radicans, also known as creeping verbena, is a perennial herb native to North America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate various health issues. The plant's extracts have been shown to possess antioxidant, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Glandularia radicans can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and support overall well-being.
